explanation: The property states that globally, whenever a power supply source change occurs (powerSupplyChanged), the system must provide a log for that power supply source change (logPowerSupply). This directly translates the requirement that the system shall provide a log to include power supply source change, including the source utilized.

The property states that globally, whenever the power supply source changes, the system shall log this change. This directly captures the requirement for the system to provide a log including power supply source changes.
The property states that globally, whenever the power supply source changes, the system shall log this change.

The natural language statement implies that logging of power supply changes must always occur when such a change happens. This is best represented by a G (Globally) operator with an implication (->). Specifically, if a 'power supply source change' occurs, then 'log power supply source change' must happen. Therefore, 'powerSupplyChanged' maps to the antecedent (bool_exp1) and 'logPowerSupply' maps to the consequent (bool_exp3) in a 'G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)' template. Template 30 precisely matches this pattern.

The property states that globally, whenever a power supply source change occurs (indicated by 'powerSupplyChanged'), a log entry for this change, including the source utilized, must be provided in the very next time step (indicated by 'X logPowerSupply'). This ensures that every power supply change is promptly and appropriately recorded, consistent with the system's logging requirement for this event.

The property states that globally (G), if a power supply source change occurs (bool_exp1), then in the very next state (X), the system shall log this power supply source change (bool_exp3). This captures the requirement for the system to provide a log immediately following a power supply source change.

The property states that globally, whenever a power supply source change is indicated (powerSupplyChanged is true), a log of that power supply source change (logPowerSupply is true) must also be provided at the same time. This is a standard response pattern where an event triggers an immediate action.

The property states that globally, whenever a power supply source change is indicated (powerSupplyChanged), a log entry for that change must also be made (logPowerSupply). This ensures that every occurrence of a power supply source change is always recorded in the log, fulfilling the requirement to 'include power supply source change, including the source utilized'.

The property states that globally, if the power supply source changes (bool_exp1), then a log of that power supply change is provided (bool_exp3). This captures the requirement that the system shall provide a log for power supply source changes, including the source utilized, by linking the occurrence of a power supply change to the action of logging it.

The property states that whenever a power supply source change occurs (indicated by 'powerSupplyChanged' becoming true), the system must, in the very next state, provide a log of this event ('logPowerSupply'). The atomic proposition 'logPowerSupply' inherently covers the detail of including the source utilized, as per its description.
